prompt: A Research Innovation Commerce & Service Management system with main features including role-based access control, marketing membership benefits, order lifecycle management, work orders for repair services, outcomes/IP registration, interaction governance, and advanced data search and analytics. The backend exposes resource-oriented API capabilities for Authentication and User/Role Management (local username + password only), supporting session/token issuance, password rotation, and fine-grained authorization down to resource type and operation; every privileged action must emit an immutable audit event capturing actor, timestamp, before/after values, and a reason code for permission changes. Membership and Marketing APIs manage member profiles, points, tiers, and promotions: points accrue at 1 point per $1.00 of eligible order subtotal, tiers default to Bronze (0–999), Silver (1,000–4,999), Gold (5,000+), and member pricing applies only when an account is in good standing. Coupon types include threshold discounts (e.g., $10 off $50), percentage discounts (max $25), and shipping waivers; campaigns include second-item discounts and spend-and-get rewards; the engine must detect conflicts using explicit priority (member price > fixed-amount coupon > percent coupon > shipping coupon) and prevent stacking beyond one coupon plus one campaign per order. Transaction & Order APIs model an order state machine from placed to confirmed-payment (recorded as internal ledger entry or cash-on-delivery confirmation), fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and completed; inventory is reserved for 30 minutes after placement, orders auto-close on timeout at 30 minutes if unpaid/unconfirmed, and duplicate-order protection requires an idempotency key unique per buyer per 10 minutes. Orders support internal notes, attachment proofs (images/video), and full list/detail queries. Work Order APIs support repair submission with text and attachments, assignment or technician claim, status progression with SLA timers (default first-response 4 business hours, resolution 3 business days), parts/cost records, follow-up ratings (1–5), and analytics/export. Outcomes/IP APIs register typed outcomes (paper, patent, competition, software copyright), link them to projects, allocate contribution shares totaling exactly 100%, archive evidence, and flag likely duplicates by normalized title plus certificate number uniqueness and abstract similarity via local token overlap thresholds. Data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Spring Boot + JPA implementing the domain services and validation rules; core tables include users, roles, permissions, audit_events (append-only), members, points_ledger, coupons, campaigns, orders, order_items, inventory, attachments, work_orders, work_order_events, outcomes, projects, contributions, comments, likes, favorites, and reports, with indexes on order_number, tracking_number, phone (stored as masked display plus encrypted full value), status, and created_at to support pagination and saved searches. Interaction governance enforces rate limits (e.g., 30 comments/hour/user; 10 reports/day/user), a local blacklist, and a sensitive-word dictionary with moderation queues and admin enforcement actions. Admin/Analytics APIs provide controlled management of users/items/orders/funds/reviews/complaints, configurable operational parameters (categories, fees, bid increments, deposit policies), KPI queries, and CSV/Excel exports generated locally. Security requirements include bcrypt password hashing, row-level permission checks on every write, encryption for sensitive fields at rest, and full offline operation (no email/SMS, no third-party logins, no cloud storage, no external APIs). The service must run as a single Docker-deployable application, meet p95 read latency under 300 ms for common queries on 100k orders, and produce structured logs plus local audit and error traces suitable for on-machine troubleshooting.
